application_structure.name: Milking Process Compliance Monitor
application_structure.note: This structure applies once the ML feasibility spike (ml-spike/) has produced a go signal per its go_no_go_criteria. It implements phases 1-5 of the delivery plan v2. Repo layout shown as a monorepo for shared tooling/contracts; split into separate repos later if deployment needs diverge.

top_level_layout.purpose: Next.js application — auth, scheduling, dashboard, statistics, reporting. Delivery plan phases 1, 2, 4, 5.
Python service — RTSP ingestion, YOLOv8 detection, task state machine, event publishing. Delivery plan phase 3.
Event and data contracts shared between web and inference-service, so both sides validate against the same schema and cannot silently drift apart.
Already built — Step 1 feasibility spike. Kept for reference; not part of the production build.
spec-file.json, delivery-plan-v2.json, and any architecture decision records (ADRs) produced during the build.
Local dev orchestration: postgres, web app, inference-service, for running the full stack together.
Documented list of required environment variables across both apps (see env_vars section below).
Root-level orientation: what each app does, how to run the stack locally, links to docs/.

shared_contracts.purpose: Single source of truth for event/data shapes so the Python inference service and the Next.js backend never disagree about a schema. Addresses the event-transport-ownership risk from the delivery plan.

files.purpose: Defines the TaskEvent shape: task_id, session_id, cow_position, confidence_score, status (completed | missed | unverifiable), detected_start/end timestamps. Consumed by inference-service to build events and by web's ingestion API to validate incoming events.
Defines cow-process boundary events (started, completed) — session_id, cow_position, timestamps.
Defines the session watch-window payload the web app sends to the inference service when a session becomes active (session_id, start_time, end_time, cow positions to watch).
Defines AuditLog entries for employee reassignment and task overrides.
States the rule explicitly: any change to a schema here requires updating both apps in the same PR. This is the enforcement mechanism against contract drift.

structure.purpose: Clerk sign-in page.
Clerk sign-up page.
Protected layout wrapping all authenticated supervisor views; enforces auth via Clerk middleware.
Multi-step session scheduling form (start time, estimated end time, cow counts, employee assignment, supervisor identity).
Session detail view: live dashboard when active, historical view when completed. Includes edit-history panel sourced from AuditLog.
Real-time monitoring view: per-cow-position task checklist, current task in progress, missed/unverifiable/completed status, manual override controls.
Historical analytics: filters by date range, employee, cow position; task duration averages; missed/unverifiable/override counts.
Report generation and download UI (single session or date-range Word export).
CRUD for sessions. POST creates a session and notifies the inference-service of a new watch-window.
PATCH endpoint for reassigning the employee on a session; writes to AuditLog.
Ingestion endpoint the inference-service publishes events to. Validates against shared-contracts schemas, persists TaskEvent/CowProcess records, broadcasts to connected dashboard clients.
structure.protection: Rate limited and authenticated via a service-to-service token, distinct from supervisor Clerk auth.

structure.purpose: PATCH endpoint for a supervisor override: writes override_status, override_reason, overridden_by, overridden_at without touching the original detected fields; writes to AuditLog.
Generates a .docx report for a session or date range, stores it, returns a download URL.
WebSocket (or SSE) upgrade endpoint for live dashboard updates, subscribed per session_id.
Step components + Zod schemas for the 4-step scheduling form.
Per-cow-position task list showing completed / missed / unverifiable states distinctly (per fairness requirement).
Modal requiring a reason before a supervisor can override a task's status.
Session window countdown/elapsed indicator, connection status for the realtime feed (with reconnect state shown to the user).
Recharts-based visualizations for task duration, missed/unverifiable rates, per-employee comparison.
shadcn/ui components.
Drizzle schema files, one per entity.
Drizzle-generated migrations.
Zod schemas for all form inputs and API request bodies (client + server shared validation).
Middleware-level rate limiting, applied especially to auth routes and the events/ingest endpoint.
Input sanitization helpers applied before persistence.
Publishes persisted events to subscribed WebSocket/SSE clients per session_id.
Builds the Word report from session/task-event data, clearly separating missed, unverifiable, and overridden outcomes.
Sends the end-of-session email via Resend/Nodemailer to the supervisor's email on record.
Calls the inference-service when a session becomes active/inactive, passing the session-window payload (per shared-contracts).
Clerk auth protection + rate limiting applied at the edge before requests reach route handlers.
FastAPI entrypoint. Exposes an endpoint to receive session-window notifications from the web app; starts/stops watching accordingly.
Connects to the camera's RTSP stream, reads frames.
Splits each frame into the two configured regions of interest (cow_position_1, cow_position_2).
Wraps the trained YOLOv8 model; runs per-ROI, per-frame detection.
Production version of the spike's state machine: tracks the six tasks per cow position, applies the confidence thresholds finalized in phase_0/phase_3, classifies completed/missed/unverifiable.
Detects cow-process start/end within the active session window, per the spec's boundary rules.
Builds TaskEvent / CowProcess event payloads conforming to shared-contracts schemas.
Publishes built events to the web app's /api/events/ingest endpoint, using the service-to-service auth token, with retry logic for transient failures.
Camera ROI coordinates for the two monitored cow positions (carried over from the spike, revalidated for the production camera install).
Finalized confidence thresholds for missed vs unverifiable classification, sourced from phase_0/phase_3 labeled-data tuning, not the spike's placeholder values.
Trained YOLOv8 weights (custom-trained on labeled milking footage, not the generic pretrained weights used in the spike).

The integration test suite required as a phase_3 acceptance gate: verifies events are correctly tagged to session_id/cow_position and correctly gated by the session window before phase_4 dashboard work is considered safe to build against.
Pinned dependencies: fastapi, ultralytics, opencv-python, uvicorn, httpx (for publishing events), pydantic (for contract validation).

build_order_mapping.note: Maps this structure back to the delivery plan v2 phases and the sequencing discussed earlier, so the agent knows what to build when.

sequence.delivery_plan_phase: phase_0 / phase_1 prerequisite — contracts must exist before either app builds against them

sequence.step: apps/web: auth shell, db schema, /api/events/ingest with a MOCKED event source, live dashboard consuming mocked events
apps/web: session scheduling flow, employee assignment + audit logging
apps/inference-service: real detection + state machine, swapped in to replace the mock

sequence.delivery_plan_phase: phase_3 acceptance gate — required before continuing phase_4 against real data

sequence.step: apps/web: overrides, statistics page, reporting, email
Hardening, threshold tuning with real pilot data
